_comment: Material library. 'density_kg_m3' is used for weight/equivalent-thickness calculations. 'mu_rho' is the NIST mass attenuation coefficient (cm^2/g) on the 'energy_MeV' grid, used by the GENERIC mono-energetic engine (B = buildup * exp(-(mu/rho)*rho*x)) for arbitrary materials and energies. Materials without a mu_rho grid still work with the modality-specific Archer/TVL data. Users may add materials or paste their own mu/rho grid. Sources: XCOM, HUBBELL (NIST).

barite_concrete.label: Barite (barytes) heavy concrete
barite_concrete.mu_rho_ref: Approximate - use density scaling of ordinary concrete or supply XCOM grid for exact barite composition.
barite_concrete.note: High-density concrete for compact vaults; for exact mu/rho enter a barite-specific XCOM grid. Density scaling of TVL: TVL_barite ~ TVL_concrete * (2350/3450) as a first approximation for the photon-dominant regime.

gypsum.label: Gypsum wallboard (CaSO4*2H2O)
gypsum.density_ref: NCRP147 (gypsum wallboard ~0.8 g/cm3 board; solid gypsum ~2.32)
gypsum.mu_rho_ref: Use NCRP147 Archer fit (archer_diagnostic.json) for diagnostic; generic mu/rho not stored.
gypsum.note: Diagnostic transmission handled by the Archer fit. Wallboard density varies; verify the board areal density for your product.

plate_glass.label: Plate glass (soda-lime)
plate_glass.mu_rho_ref: Use NCRP147 Archer fit for diagnostic; generic mu/rho not stored.

lead_glass.label: Lead glass (leaded viewing window)
lead_glass.density_ref: Typical 4.8-6.2 g/cm3 depending on PbO content; verify product spec (lead-equivalence usually quoted in mm Pb).
lead_glass.mu_rho_ref: Manufacturer lead-equivalence preferred; or supply XCOM grid.
lead_glass.note: Lead-glass windows are normally specified by lead equivalence (mm Pb) at a stated kVp - enter that directly as a lead layer for design.

wood.label: Wood (soft)
wood.mu_rho_ref: Use NCRP147 Archer fit for diagnostic; generic mu/rho not stored.

brick.label: Brick (clay, solid)
brick.mu_rho_ref: Approximate by ordinary concrete mu/rho (similar effective Z) or supply XCOM grid.
brick.note: For screening, brick is often approximated using concrete mu/rho scaled by density. Verify mortar/void fraction for a real wall.
